title: Local rule evaluation with write-time enforcement
description:
Evaluate local rules on every issue mutation path (create, update, batch), enforce blocking rules, and log `--force` bypasses.

## Background

Issue-scoped rules must run wherever an issue is written, including the separate batch path, or `enforce` rules are trivially bypassable. Graph-scope rules are skipped on write. See `dev/active/f6a704d0-validation-engine-plan.md` (step 5) and decision record sections 7.5 and 7.6.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] [hard] Local rules evaluate in `issue.rs` create and update, replacing the current IssueValidator call site.
- [ ] [hard] Local rules ALSO evaluate in `bulk_update.rs` `validate_update()` (the batch path), so `jit issue update --filter` cannot bypass enforce rules.
- [ ] [hard] An `error` finding from an `enforce=true` rule blocks the write; `--force` bypasses it.
- [ ] [hard] A `--force` bypass of an enforce rule appends a new `Event` variant to `events.jsonl` (rule name, issue id); ordinary rejections and read-only validate runs are not logged.
- [ ] [hard] Graph-scope rules are skipped on the write path.

## Notes

Anchors: `issue.rs` create call site 54-63, update 227-236; `bulk_update.rs` `validate_update` 416-468; `Event` enum at `domain/types.rs:640-791` with helpers near 795; append via `storage.append_event`.
